Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Actinopterygii; Neopterygii; Teleostei; Neoteleostei; Acanthomorphata; Eupercaria; Tetraodontiformes; Monacanthidae; Monacanthus
Description: Monacanthus ciliatus genome assembly, chromosome: 11
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Actinopterygii; Neopterygii; Teleostei; Neoteleostei; Acanthomorphata; Eupercaria; Tetraodontiformes; Monacanthidae; Monacanthus
Assembly: The assembly of Monacanthus ciliatus (fMonCil1) is based on 34X ONT data and 225X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Fly, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, no contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 317 regions totaling 8.4 Mb (with the largest being 0.229 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, no supplementary haplotypic regions were removed. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Turdidae; Luscinia
Description: Luscinia svecica genome assembly, chromosome: 18
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Turdidae; Luscinia
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bLusSve1 of the Bluethroat. The sample was provided by Arild Johnsen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bLusSve1 is based on 30x PromethION R10 Oxford Nanopore data and 55x Arima Hi-C data. The R10 ONT reads were assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been added to hap1.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Discoba; Euglenozoa; Euglenida; Spirocuta; Euglenophyceae; Eutreptiales; Eutreptiaceae; Eutreptiella
Description: Eutreptiella sp. 'Long Island Sound 2002' genome assembly, chromosome: 18
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Discoba; Euglenozoa; Euglenida; Spirocuta; Euglenophyceae; Eutreptiales; Eutreptiaceae; Eutreptiella
Assembly: The assembly peEutLong1.2 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and plastid genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (0 - 10.3 Mbp) and chromosome 5 (39.1 - 52.9 Mbp) are unknown.
